Localization.Description: Possible forced user authentication using .scf or .url files. Such authentication allows attackers to obtain NTLM password hashes of users who open a local folder or network share with these files using Windows Explorer. Attackers can then use these hashes for attacks such as Pass-the-Hash, or to bruteforce passwords in clear text and use them to gain access to other systems.

EventDescriptions.EventDescription: A user from host {src.host} was redirected for forced authentication to host {dst.host} after opening the folder with placed by user {subject.account.name} malicious file "{object.name}" on network share of host {event_src.host}
Possible forced authentication of users opening a network share with file "{object.storage.name}" (placed by user {subject.account.name} from host {src.host}) on host {event_src.host}

User {subject.account.name} from host {src.host} was redirected for forced authentication to host {dst.host} after downloading file "{object.name}" and opening the folder with this file on host {event_src.host}
Possible forced authentication of user {subject.account.name} after downloading file "{object.name}" and opening the folder with this file on host {event_src.host}
